attempts.test_code:
import pytest
from source import wrap

def test_wrap_trailing_words():
    """
    The wrap function must include any remaining text after the loop.
    The buggy implementation omits the final part, causing the test
    to fail. After the fix the returned list should contain the last
    word(s) as its final element.
    """
    input_text = "aaa bbb ccc"
    width = 4
    expected = ["aaa", "bbb", "ccc"]
    result = wrap(input_text, width)
    assert result == expected, f"Expected {expected}, got {result}"

attempts.test_code:
import pytest
from source import wrap

def test_wrap_trailing_words():
    text = "aaa bbb ccc"
    width = 4
    result = wrap(text, width)
    assert len(result) == 3, f"Expected 3 lines, got {len(result)}"
    assert result[-1] == " ccc", f"Expected last line to be ' ccc', got {result[-1]}"
